summary: Gruber's 2024 Apple Report Card reveals a year where no category improved — every changed grade went lower. He gives high marks to Mac and iPhone (both A) for sustained excellence, but downgrades Apple Watch, Apple TV, Home, Developer Relations, and Social Impact. His sharpest criticism targets Siri/Apple Intelligence, which he'd grade a near-failing D if it were its own category, arguing Apple shipped features a year too early under competitive pressure. He notes that developer resentment over App Store policies is worsening and hurting VisionOS adoption, and criticizes Tim Cook's political maneuvering around the Trump inauguration as transparently self-serving.
key_points: Not a single category improved year-over-year — every grade that changed went down, suggesting Apple is coasting on past excellence without leveling up anywhere
Siri/Apple Intelligence deserves its own report card category and would earn a near-failing grade; Apple shipped AI features a year early out of competitive panic rather than readiness
Developer relations continue to deteriorate, now graded D, with App Store resentment directly dampening third-party enthusiasm for VisionOS
Apple TV+ content quality is the one bright spot — Gruber calls it 'the new HBO' — but it's worrisome that Apple's clearest improvement is unrelated to its core computing business
Tim Cook's $1 million Trump inauguration donation versus $43,200 for Biden's is a 'hoagie-sized shit sandwich' that undermines Apple's carefully cultivated social values
money_quotes: There are several areas where Apple maintained consistent excellence in 2024, but I can't think of a single area where Apple levelled up. Not one. That feels like a problem.
Apple is simply not a relevant player in the explosively popular LLM game.
It does matter that Siri sucks because only Siri can tightly integrate at the system level with your device, and with your private cross-application data.
Trying to work on an iPad, for me, continues to feel like trying to perform surgery while wearing mittens.
Changes for the sake of change are the surest sign of a company in decline.
Tim Cook can't donate $1 million to Trump's inauguration slush fund when Apple donated a mere $43,200 to Biden's and expect us to do anything but laugh at the supposed justification.
That's Tim Cook's hoagie-sized shit sandwich to eat, and it's disrespectful to pitch us on taking a few bites ourselves to help him get through it.
Excellence, no matter how long sustained, is always precarious — delicate and fragile — and on the verge of succumbing to hubris and Father Time.
key_insight: Apple's 2024 was a year of maintained but unchallenged excellence across its lineup, with no single area improving — a stagnation made most dangerous by its failure to compete meaningfully in AI and its deteriorating relationship with developers.
